symptom: one arm and hand that had grown shorter and more crooked than the other
hook: the bones looked like they were stuffed with translucent cartilage balloons mostly on one side
whatYouSee: Numerous lucent expansile cartilage lesions crowd one limb asymmetrically and leave it distorted and shortened.
whyItMatters: This is multiple enchondromatosis without the hemangiomas of Maffucci; it causes limb deformity and length discrepancy and carries a real risk of chondrosarcoma.
treatment: Management is orthopaedic correction of deformity and length discrepancy plus long-term monitoring for malignant transformation.
takeaway: Many cartilage lesions crowding one side of the body is Ollier disease. It is the hemangioma-free cousin of Maffucci.

condition.symptom: one arm and hand that had grown shorter and more crooked than the other
condition.hook: the bones looked like they were stuffed with translucent cartilage balloons mostly on one side
condition.keyFindings: multiple expansile lucent enchondromas with a typically unilateral or asymmetric distribution, causing bone expansion, deformity and shortening, often with linear striations

condition.takeaway: Many cartilage lesions crowding one side of the body is Ollier disease. It is the hemangioma-free cousin of Maffucci.

👀 What you see:
Numerous lucent expansile cartilage lesions crowd one limb asymmetrically and leave it distorted and shortened.

🦴 Why it matters:
This is multiple enchondromatosis without the hemangiomas of Maffucci; it causes limb deformity and length discrepancy and carries a real risk of chondrosarcoma.

📝 Takeaway:
Many cartilage lesions crowding one side of the body is Ollier disease. It is the hemangioma-free cousin of Maffucci.
